intro: Diplopappus caucasicus, commonly known as Caucasian dandelion or Caucasus hawk's beard, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, offering potential health benefits in treating digestive issues, fever, and skin problems. The bioactive constituents of Diplopappus caucasicus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used to make teas and tinctures for the treatment of various ailments.
